vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1813: pan plan finalize — bd timeouts under load half-finalize the plan (timed-out creates lose bead IDs and silently drop dependency edges)

plan.title: Make pan plan finalize resilient to bd timeouts under multi-agent load
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: Under multi-agent Dolt contention (~15 agents, load ~10) a single bd op routinely exceeds the fixed timeouts hardcoded in src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts (list 15s, delete 10s, ping 8s, create 30s). The failure handling makes a timeout worse than a clean failure: a `bd create` that times out client-side usually SUCCEEDED server-side, so its bead ID is never captured and every dependent bead is created WITHOUT its --deps — a silently corrupted dependency DAG. Other timed-out reads (dedup list, post-delete list) abort the whole finalize, and because plan.status is flipped to 'proposed' BEFORE beads are created, a mid-run failure leaves a half-finalized state needing manual reconciliation. Observed 4x consecutively during PAN-1807 finalize, each failing differently.
narratives.Proposal: Make a single finalize run SURVIVE bd contention via five independently reviewable changes, without altering the cross-process contention model: (1) adaptive/configurable bd timeout — clamp(bd-ping total_ms × 20, floor 30s, ceiling 180s) with PANOPTICON_BD_TIMEOUT_MS override, routed through all bd calls in beads.ts; (2) bounded exponential-backoff retry for idempotent bd reads (ping/list/delete) — create excluded; (3) recover a bead's ID by exact-title re-query after a create timeout, before declaring failure, so dependents keep their edges; (4) verify dependency edges as a deterministic post-condition (one batched `bd dep list --json`) and repair missing ones with `bd dep add`, failing finalize loudly if a repair fails; (5) stamp plan.status='proposed' only AFTER beads creation succeeds, so a failure leaves the spec cleanly re-runnable.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Eliminating cross-process Dolt lock contention (no lock daemon, no Dolt config change, no global cross-agent bd queue) — these changes make one finalize survive contention, not reduce systemic load.
- Client-generated explicit bead IDs (`bd create --id`) — rejected for prefix-collision risk; re-query-by-title is used instead.
- A true distributed transaction across bd + spec file + the HTTP promote phase — phases are made idempotent/re-runnable and ordered for recoverable partial-failure states, not 2-phase-committed.
- Changing the complete-planning HTTP promote behavior (already fails gracefully).
- Touching bd timeouts in any file other than src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts (and the stamp reorder in src/cli/commands/plan-finalize.ts).
- Removing the existing 3-consecutive-failure verification bypass or any unrelated pipeline gate.

autoDecisions.summary: Recover bead IDs by exact-title re-query after a create timeout rather than using `bd create --id`.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue's own suggested fix; client-generated IDs risk prefix collisions with the shared Dolt DB and change the ID scheme. Re-query-by-title recovers the only thing lost on a server-side-successful create — the printed ID — and stays within the existing model.

autoDecisions.summary: Adaptive timeout = clamp(bd-ping total_ms × 20, floor 30000ms, ceiling 180000ms), env override PANOPTICON_BD_TIMEOUT_MS wins absolutely.
autoDecisions.rationale: Floor preserves today's 30s so an idle box is unchanged (no regression). The ping probe itself waits on the Dolt lock, so its latency already reflects contention — multiplying it scales the timeout to observed load. A hard ceiling guarantees no unbounded wait.

autoDecisions.summary: Retry only idempotent bd reads (ping, dedup list, post-delete list, delete); never auto-retry bd create.
autoDecisions.rationale: bd create is not idempotent — a retry after a server-side-successful create produces a duplicate bead. Reads are safe to re-issue and recover Runs 3 and 4 (transient list/ping timeouts).

autoDecisions.summary: An unrepairable dependency edge FAILS the finalize (non-zero exit) rather than warning.
autoDecisions.rationale: A corrupt DAG is the exact bug being fixed; failing loudly and re-running is strictly better than materializing a plan whose wave scheduling runs beads out of order.

autoDecisions.summary: No contradictions between issue body and any linked artifact were found; planning proceeded end-to-end in --auto mode.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue is a self-contained bug report with concrete root causes and suggested fixes; nothing required human disambiguation.

items.title: Adaptive, configurable bd op timeout routed through all bd calls in beads.ts

narrative.Action: Add a resolveBdTimeout(workspacePath) helper to src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts. Behavior: if process.env.PANOPTICON_BD_TIMEOUT_MS parses to a positive integer, return it verbatim WITHOUT probing. Otherwise run `bd ping --json` once (using a fixed bootstrap timeout constant BD_PING_BOOTSTRAP_TIMEOUT_MS = 8000 — the resolver must NOT call itself), read total_ms from the JSON, and return Math.min(180000, Math.max(30000, total_ms * 20)). If the ping throws or its JSON is unparseable, return the floor 30000 (never throw). Cache the resolved value for the lifetime of one createBeadsFromVBriefPromise call so the probe runs once per finalize. Replace the hardcoded operational timeout literals — bd list (beads.ts:80), bd delete (:98), bd create (:382), and the init/doctor/config recovery ops (:179,:183,:220,:238,:242,:206,:230) — with the resolved value. Leave the non-bd `which bd` check (:155, timeout 5000) and the resolver's own bootstrap ping unchanged.

items.title: Given PANOPTICON_BD_TIMEOUT_MS=120000, resolveBdTimeout returns exactly 120000 and does NOT invoke bd ping (assert mockExecAsync received no ping call).
With no env override: bd ping total_ms=5000 ⇒ resolveBdTimeout returns 100000; total_ms=255 ⇒ returns 30000 (clamped to floor); total_ms=12000 ⇒ returns 180000 (clamped to ceiling).
When bd ping throws or returns unparseable JSON, resolveBdTimeout returns 30000 and does not throw.

The bd create call in beads.ts passes the resolved timeout — a unit test asserts the timeout option mockExecAsync received for `bd create` equals the resolved value, and grep finds no remaining 15000/10000/30000 literal on the list/delete/create calls.
Bounded exponential-backoff retry for idempotent bd reads (create excluded)

narrative.Action: Add a retryBd(fn, { attempts = 3, baseDelayMs = 500 }) wrapper in src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts that retries fn on failure with exponential backoff (delay = baseDelayMs * 2^(attempt-1)) and surfaces the last error after attempts are exhausted. Apply it to the idempotent bd ops in the create/dedup path ONLY: the connectivity bd ping (beads.ts:205/:229), the dedup bd list (listBeadsForIssue at :77, used by clearBeadsForIssue), the post-delete bd list (:108 path), and bd delete (:97). Do NOT wrap bd create. Sleep via `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, ms))` (no execSync sleep).

items.title: Given the post-delete bd list times out on attempts 1 and 2 then succeeds on attempt 3, clearBeadsForIssue completes with no error (reproduces and recovers issue Run 3).
Given bd list times out on all 3 attempts, the operation fails surfacing the timeout error and issues no 4th attempt (assert mockExecAsync list-call count === 3).
Given a single bd create timeout, no second create is issued — bd create is never wrapped by retryBd (assert mockExecAsync create-call count === 1).
The backoff unit test drives delays with vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync() and contains no real-time waits (no maxForks:1 workaround).
Recover a bead ID by exact-title re-query after a create timeout

narrative.Action: In the create loop of createBeadsFromVBriefPromise (src/lib/vbrief/beads.ts ~:378-402), when `bd create` throws (timeout) OR returns empty stdout, recover the ID before recording failure: re-query `bd list --json -l <issueLabel>` and select the bead whose title strictly equals the full title `${plan.id}: ${item.title}`. If exactly one matches, set beadIds.set(itemId, recoveredId) and push(fullTitle) into created, then continue so later items' --deps resolve. If none match, record the failure as today (genuine create failure). If more than one matches, push an explicit ambiguity error and do NOT set beadIds for that item. The re-query should use the adaptive timeout from resolveBdTimeout (and may use retryBd).

items.title: Given a bd create that times out but a subsequent bd list returns a bead whose title equals `${plan.id}: ${item.title}`, beadIds gains that item's recovered ID and a dependent item created afterward includes the recovered ID in its --deps argument.
Given a bd create timeout AND the re-query returns no bead with the matching full title, the item is recorded in errors and beadIds has no entry for it.
Given the re-query returns two beads with the matching full title, the function pushes an ambiguity error naming the title and does NOT set beadIds for that item (no silent pick).
Given a bd create that returns a non-empty bead ID, the normal path is taken and no recovery `bd list` is issued for that item (assert no list call follows a successful create).
Verify and repair dependency edges after creation

narrative.Action: After the create loop in createBeadsFromVBriefPromise, add a post-condition verification: if plan.edges contains any `blocks` edge and beadIds is non-empty, issue ONE batched `bd dep list <beadId…> --json` over all created bead IDs (records carry issue_id = the dependent bead and id = the blocker it depends on, with dependency_type 'blocks'). Build the expected set from plan.edges (edge from→to means bead(to) depends on bead(from), i.e. expect a dep record with issue_id=bead(to), id=bead(from)). For each expected edge whose both endpoints were created but is absent from the actual set, repair it with `bd dep add <bead(to)> <bead(from)>`. After repairs, push an error for any expected edge that is still absent so createBeadsFromVBrief returns success:false (finalize exits non-zero). Edges whose endpoints were not both captured are reported as errors, not repaired. Use resolveBdTimeout for the dep-list/dep-add calls.

items.title: Given a plan with edge A→B where B was created without the --deps to A (simulated), finalize issues `bd dep add <beadB> <beadA>` and createBeadsFromVBrief returns success:true.
Given the batched bd dep list already contains every plan.edges blocks edge, no `bd dep add` call is issued and createBeadsFromVBrief returns success:true.
Given a missing edge whose `bd dep add` repair fails, createBeadsFromVBrief returns success:false with an error naming the unrepaired edge.
Given a plan with zero `blocks` edges, the verification step issues no bd dep list and no bd dep add calls and createBeadsFromVBrief returns success:true.
Stamp plan.status=proposed only after beads creation succeeds

narrative.Action: In planFinalizeCommand (src/cli/commands/plan-finalize.ts), move the `const canonicalFilename = stampPlanForFinalization(planPath, issueId)` call (currently line 167, before createBeadsFromVBrief at line 173) to AFTER the beads-success check (after line 194, before promotePlanning at line 225). On the beads-failure exit path (lines 176-190) the stamp must NOT run, so the workspace .pan/spec.vbrief.json keeps its pre-finalize plan.status. Keep canonicalFilename in scope for the success-path JSON/text output and ensure emitAutoPromotePhase ordering stays coherent. Do not change stampPlanForFinalization itself.

items.title: Given createBeadsFromVBrief fails, the workspace spec plan.status is NOT changed to 'proposed' (retains its pre-finalize value) and planFinalizeCommand exits non-zero.
Given createBeadsFromVBrief succeeds, plan.status is set to 'proposed' and canonicalFilename is stamped before promotePlanning is invoked (unchanged success-path behavior).
Re-running pan plan finalize after a prior beads failure produces one clean finalize — dedup clears any partial beads and plan.status flips to 'proposed' exactly once, with no manual reconciliation.
